name: happy-path-variant-b
task: Add a new domain for options chain data. Users should be able to query the options chain for any ticker, filtered by expiration date. Include both calls and puts.
assertions: Response creates a domain directory named 'options' or 'options_chain' (snake_case, not camelCase or PascalCase)
The client file validates that the required API key environment variable exists before making requests
Response defines TypeScript types or interfaces for the options chain response data (strike, expiration, type/side, bid, ask, or similar fields)
The tool registry entry description mentions options, calls, puts, or expiration — making it unambiguous for LLM tool selection
The router includes error handling that converts exceptions to appropriate error responses (not raw stack traces)
